enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Chapter I - Preliminary
Note: Mere mention of an activity, transaction or item in these Directions does
not imply that it is permitted, and the bank shall refer to the extant statutory and
regulatory requirements while determining the permissibility or otherwise of an
activity, transaction, or item.
enriched_requirements.object: statutory and regulatory requirements while determining the permissibility or otherwise of an activity, transaction, or item

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks
- Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect from the date of issue.
enriched_requirements.object: the date of issue

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Small Finance Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
enriched_requirements.object: Finance Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Business Day' means the opening hours of the Large Value Payment
System (LVPS) or of correspondent banking services during which a bank
can receive and make payments in a local jurisdiction.
enriched_requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Business Day' means the opening hours of the Large Value Payment System (LVPS) or of correspondent bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Cash in hand' shall consist of total amount of notes and coins held by
bank branches / ATMs / Cash deposit machines maintained by a bank,
including transit cash on bank's books as also cash with Business

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of Directors (Board) shall be responsible for overall management of
liquidity risk, and shall decide the strategy, policies, and procedures of the bank
to manage liquidity risk in accordance with the liquidity risk tolerance / limits.
enriched_requirements.object: management of liquidity risk, and shall decide the strategy, policies, and procedures of the bank to manage liquidity risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall have a clear understanding of the bank's liquidity risk, including
the liquidity risk profile of all its branches, subsidiaries, and associates (both
domestic and overseas).

management.
enriched_requirements.object: is understood at all levels of management

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall establish executive level authority and responsibility for liquidity
risk management, enforce management's duties to identify, measure, monitor,
and manage liquidity risk and formulate / review the Contingent Funding Plan
(CFP).

enriched_requirements.object: approval of policies, strategies, and procedures to manage liquidity risk, and review them at least annually

The Board shall also approve the policy regarding inclusion and
monitoring of only those intraday liquidity sources which are freely and readily
available to the bank at the start of the day.
enriched_requirements.object: regarding inclusion and monitoring of only those intraday liquidity sources which are freely and readily available to the bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the internal limits for liquidity stock ratios, based on its

enriched_requirements.object: for liquidity stock ratios, based on its liquidity risk management capabilities, experience, and risk profile

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It shall also
set an explicit liquidity risk tolerance, which shall define the level of liquidity risk
that the bank is willing to assume and reflect the bank's financial condition and
funding capacity.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity risk tolerance, which shall define the level of liquidity risk that the bank is willing to assume and reflect the bank'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall review information necessary to maintain its understanding of
liquidity risk, the key assumptions used in setting the liquidity risk tolerance, and
contingency plans for their effectiveness and operational feasibility at least on an
annual basis.
enriched_requirements.object: maintain its understanding of liquidity risk, the key assumptions used in setting the liquidity risk tolerance, and co

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board or Risk Management Committee (RMC) shall inter-alia approve the
internal prudential limits for cumulative mismatches across all time buckets of
structural liquidity statement (SLS) for monitoring by the bank, as also approve
appropriate internal limits on Earnings at Risk (EaR) and volatility in the Market
Value of Equity (MVE), based on bank's risk bearing and risk management
capacity.
enriched_requirements.object: prudential limits for cumulative mismatches across all time buckets of structural liquidity statement (SLS) for monito

The Board / ALCO shall periodically review the internal limits after assessing

of Net Interest Income (NII) and volatility in Net Worth.
enriched_requirements.object: limits after assessing various scenarios of interest rates and the resultant volatility of earnings in terms of Net I

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A reliable Management Information System (MIS) designed to provide timely and
forward-looking information on the liquidity position of the bank and the banking
group, under normal and stress situations, shall be presented before the Board
and the Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO).
enriched_requirements.object: Board and the Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any vulnerability observed in the stress test results shall be reported to the Board
and the Board shall ensure that the bank designs a plan of action to address the
vulnerability immediately.
enriched_requirements.object: Board and the Board shall ensure that the bank designs a plan of action to address the vulnerability immediately

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall ensure that the bank:
(1) develops and adopts a suitable intraday liquidity risk management strategy
that enables the bank to:
enriched_requirements.object: (1) develops and adopts a suitable intraday liquidity risk management strategy that enables the bank to:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the risk tolerance of individual business lines for the
bank to align the risk-taking incentives and the liquidity risk exposure of each
business line.
enriched_requirements.object: of individual business lines for the bank to align the risk-taking incentives and the liquidity risk exposure of each busin

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Successful implementation of liquidity risk management process shall emanate
from the Top Management in a bank with the demonstration of its strong
commitment to integrate basic operations and strategic decision making with risk
management.
enriched_requirements.object: Management in a bank with the demonstration of its strong commitment to integrate basic operations and strategic decision making

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The organisational set up for liquidity risk management shall be as
under:
(1) Board of Directors (Board)
(2) Risk Management Committee (RMC)
(3) Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO)
(4) Asset Liability Management (ALM) Support Group
<image>
enriched_requirements.object: Board of Directors (Board) (2) Risk Management Committee (RMC) (3) Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO) (4) Asset Liability Ma

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The Board shall be responsible for overall management of liquidity risk.
enriched_requirements.object: management of liquidity risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
Board shall decide the strategy, policies and procedures of the bank to manage
liquidity risk in accordance with the liquidity risk tolerance / limits as detailed in
paragraphs 46 to 48 .
enriched_requirements.object: and procedures of the bank to manage liquidity risk in accordance with the liquidity risk tolerance / limits as detailed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall ensure that the risk tolerance is clearly
understood at all levels of management.
enriched_requirements.object: tolerance is clearly understood at all levels of management

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall also ensure that it
understands the nature of the liquidity risk of the bank including liquidity risk
profile of all branches, and associates, periodically reviews information
necessary to maintain this understanding, establishes executive-level lines of
authority and responsibility for managing the bank's liquidity risk, enforces
management's duties to identify, measure, monitor, and manage liquidity risk and
formulates / reviews the CFP.
enriched_requirements.object: understands the nature of the liquidity risk of the bank including liquidity risk profile of all branches, and associates, periodi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The RMC, which reports to the Board, consisting of Chief Executive Officer
(CEO) / Chairman and Managing Director (CMD) and heads of credit, market,
and operational risk management committee, shall be responsible for evaluating
liquidity risk faced by a bank.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity risk faced by a bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The RMC shall also include the potential interaction
of liquidity risk with the other risks addressed by it.
enriched_requirements.object: interaction of liquidity risk with the other risks addressed by it

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall be responsible for ensuring adherence to the risk tolerance /
limits set by the Board as well as implementing the liquidity risk management
strategy of the bank in line with a bank's decided risk management objectives
and risk tolerance.
enriched_requirements.object: adherence to the risk tolerance / limits set by the Board as well as implementing the liquidity risk management strategy o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall be headed by the
CEO / CMD or the Executive Director (ED) of a bank.
enriched_requirements.object: CEO / CMD or the Executive Director (ED) of a bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO members may
include the Heads of Investment, Credit and Strategy, Treasury, International
Banking, Risk Management, and Economic Research and other members as
deemed suitable.
enriched_requirements.object: Investment, Credit and Strategy, Treasury, International Banking, Risk Management, and Economic Research and other members as dee

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall perform the following functions with respect to the liquidity risk
of the bank:
(1) Decide on desired maturity profile and mix of incremental assets and
liabilities.
enriched_requirements.object: with respect to the liquidity risk of the bank: (1) Decide on desired maturity profile and mix of incremental assets a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Towards this end,
it shall develop a view on future direction of interest rate movements and
<image>
decide on funding mix between fixed v/s.
enriched_requirements.object: future direction of interest rate movements and <image> decide on funding mix between fixed v/s

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall be aware of the composition,
characteristics, and diversification of a bank's assets and funding sources
and shall regularly review the funding strategy in the light of any changes
in the internal or external environments.
enriched_requirements.object: composition, characteristics, and diversification of a bank's assets and funding sources and shall regularly review the funding strate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall have a thorough understanding of the close links between
Funding Liquidity Risk and Market Liquidity Risk, as well as how other risks
including credit, market, operational, and reputational risks affect the bank's
overall liquidity risk strategy.
enriched_requirements.object: of the close links between Funding Liquidity Risk and Market Liquidity Risk, as well as how other risks including credit

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall identify events that could have an
impact on market and public perceptions about bank's soundness and
reputation.
enriched_requirements.object: have an impact on market and public perceptions about bank's soundness and reputation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALM Support Group consisting of operating staff shall be responsible for
analysing, monitoring, and reporting the liquidity risk profile to the ALCO.
enriched_requirements.object: monitoring, and reporting the liquidity risk profile to the ALCO

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
group shall prepare forecasts and simulations to assess the impact of various
possible changes in market conditions on a bank's liquidity position and
recommend action needed to be taken to maintain the liquidity position and
ensure adherence to bank's internal limits.
enriched_requirements.object: to assess the impact of various possible changes in market conditions on a bank's liquidity position and recommend a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place an effective liquidity risk management policy, which inter
alia, shall spell out the liquidity risk tolerance, funding strategies, prudential limits,
system for measuring, assessing and reporting / reviewing liquidity, framework
for stress testing, liquidity planning under alternative scenarios / formal
contingent funding plan, nature and frequency of management reporting and
periodical review of assumptions used in liquidity projection.
enriched_requirements.object: effective liquidity risk management policy, which inter alia, shall spell out the liquidity risk tolerance, funding strategies, pruden

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also
address liquidity risk for individual currencies;
enriched_requirements.object: for individual currencies

conditions: when appropriate and material
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and business lines, when appropriate and material,
and shall place limits on transfer of liquidity keeping in view the regulatory, legal
and operational constraints.
enriched_requirements.object: of liquidity keeping in view the regulatory, legal and operational constraints

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board or its delegated committee of board members shall oversee the
establishment and approval of policies, strategies and procedures to manage
liquidity risk, and review them at least annually.
enriched_requirements.object: approval of policies, strategies and procedures to manage liquidity risk, and review them at least annually

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have a sound process for identifying, measuring, monitoring, and
mitigating, along with a strategy for liquidity risk management.
enriched_requirements.object: for identifying, measuring, monitoring, and mitigating, along with a strategy for liquidity risk management

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The strategy for managing liquidity risk shall be appropriate for the nature, scale,
and complexity of a bank's activities.
enriched_requirements.object: nature, scale, and complexity of a bank's activities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In formulating the strategy, a bank / banking
Group shall take into consideration its legal structures, key business lines, the
breadth and diversity of markets and products.
enriched_requirements.object: legal structures, key business lines, the breadth and diversity of markets and products

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

sources of funding to meet daily operating cash outflows, as well as expected
and unexpected cash flow (inflow / outflow) fluctuations.
enriched_requirements.object: funding to meet daily operating cash outflows, as well as expected and unexpected cash flow (inflow / outflow) fluctuation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall define and identify the liquidity risk to which it is exposed for each

enriched_requirements.object: liquidity risk to which it is exposed for each major on and off-balance sheet (OBS) position

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The identification process shall

enriched_requirements.object: embedded options and other contingent exposures that may affect the bank's sources and uses of funds

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Liquidity risk shall be identified for
all currencies in which a bank is active.
enriched_requirements.object: currencies in which a bank is active

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank
shall adopt the format of the structural liquidity statement (SLS), provided in
enriched_requirements.object: the structural liquidity statement (SLS)

placed in different time buckets based on the residual maturity of the cash flows
or the projected future behaviour of assets, liabilities, and OBS items.
enriched_requirements.object: time buckets based on the residual maturity of the cash flows or the projected future behaviour of assets, liabilities, and OBS

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
difference between cash inflows and outflows in each time period shall be starting

series of points of time.
enriched_requirements.object: the measurement of the bank's future liquidity surplus or deficit, at a series of points of time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall prepare domestic SLS (₹) on a daily basis and report to RBI on a
fortnightly basis.
enriched_requirements.object: on a daily basis and report to RBI on a fortnightly basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
SLS shall include four parts:
(1) Domestic Currency - Indian Operations
(2) Foreign Currency - Indian Operations
(3) Combined Indian Operations - Domestic and Foreign Currency, i.e., solo
bank level
(4) For Consolidated Bank Operations
The guidance for slotting the future cash flows of a bank in the time buckets has
been provided in Annex-V.
enriched_requirements.object: Domestic Currency - Indian Operations (2) Foreign Currency - Indian Operations (3) Combined Indian Operations - Domestic and F

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall analyse the behavioural maturity profile of various components of
on / off-balance sheet items on the basis of assumptions and trend analysis
supported by time series analysis.
enriched_requirements.object: profile of various components of on / off-balance sheet items on the basis of assumptions and trend analysis supporte

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The behavioural analysis, for example, may
include the proportion of maturing assets and liabilities that the bank can rollover

dates, potential cash flows from OBS activities, including draw down under loan
commitments, contingent liabilities and market related transactions.
enriched_requirements.object: maturing assets and liabilities that the bank can rollover or renew, the behaviour of assets and liabilities with no clearly

enriched_requirements.object: least once in six months to validate the assumptions used in the behavioral analysis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

enriched_requirements.object: a period which facilitate near reality predictions about future behaviour of on / off-balance sheet items

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

options after specified times.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The assumptions used in projections of cash flows and measuring liquidity risk,

enriched_requirements.object: adequately documented

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
They shall also
be periodically reviewed by the Board / RMC.
enriched_requirements.object: by the Board / RMC

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: A bank may consider measures / ratios in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: in this regard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: A bank may also use other measures / ratios.
enriched_requirements.object: / ratios

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may monitor liquidity risk by putting in place internally defined limits

enriched_requirements.object: putting in place internally defined limits approved by the Board for such measures / ratios

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such limits should be based
on its liquidity risk management capabilities, experience, and risk profile.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity risk management capabilities, experience, and risk profile

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such
measures / ratios may be used to monitor the liquidity risk in ₹ and in major
currencies at the solo bank level.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank may refer to the following guidance for computation of above
ratios:
(1) Volatile Liabilities: (i) (Deposits + borrowings and bills payable up to 1
year).

While the mismatches in SLS up to one year would be relevant, since these

enriched_requirements.object: mismatches, i

monitor cumulative mismatches across all time buckets by setting internal
prudential limits, with the approval of the Board or the RMC.

The net cumulative negative mismatches in the domestic SLS (Refer Annex-I
Part A1 Liquidity Return) during the next day, 2-7 days, 8-14 days, and 15-30
days bucket shall not exceed 5 per cent, 10 per cent, 15 per cent, and 20 per
<image>
cent of the cumulative cash outflows in the respective time bucket.
enriched_requirements.object: 10 per cent, 15 per cent, and 20 per <image> cent of the cumulative cash outflows in the respective time bucket

bank operations (Annex-I Part B).
enriched_requirements.object: cumulative mismatch limits for its SLS for consolidated bank operations (Annex-I Part B)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have a measurement, monitoring and control system for liquidity
positions in the major currencies in which it is active.
enriched_requirements.object: and control system for liquidity positions in the major currencies in which it is active

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For assessing the liquidity

enriched_requirements.object: Liquidity Return as provided in Annex-I

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For slotting the various items of assets and liabilities in

enriched_requirements.object: provided in Annex-V

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to
assessing its aggregate foreign currency liquidity needs and the acceptable
mismatch in combination with its domestic currency commitments, a bank shall

individually by taking into account the outcome of stress testing.
enriched_requirements.object: of its strategy for each major currency individually by taking into account the outcome of stress testing

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The assessment of foreign currency mismatches by a bank shall take into
account:
(1) bank's ability to raise funds in foreign currency markets.
enriched_requirements.object: bank's ability to raise funds in foreign currency markets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank's Board shall set an explicit liquidity risk tolerance, which shall define the
level of liquidity risk that a bank is willing to assume and reflect its financial
condition and funding capacity.
enriched_requirements.object: risk tolerance, which shall define the level of liquidity risk that a bank is willing to assume and reflect its financial co

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The tolerance should ensure that the bank
manages its liquidity in normal times in such a way that it is able to withstand a

enriched_requirements.object: manages its liquidity in normal times in such a way that it is able to withstand a prolonged period of both institution specific

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The articulation of risk tolerance shall be explicit, comprehensive, and
appropriate as per bank's complexity, business mix, liquidity risk profile, and
<image>
systemic significance;
enriched_requirements.object: appropriate as per bank's complexity, business mix, liquidity risk profile, and <image> systemic significance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of mismatches in cash flows in the near-term buckets up to 30
days, a bank shall endeavour to keep the cash flow mismatches at the minimum
levels.
enriched_requirements.object: cash flow mismatches at the minimum levels

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Risk tolerance may also be specified for various ratios under stock
approach.
enriched_requirements.object: various ratios under stock approach

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Liquidity risk tolerance may also be expressed in terms of minimum survival
horizons under a range of severe but plausible stress scenarios, chosen to reflect
the particular vulnerabilities of the bank.
enriched_requirements.object: terms of minimum survival horizons under a range of severe but plausible stress scenarios, chosen to reflect the particular vulne

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall periodically review the
key assumptions.
enriched_requirements.object: assumptions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall maintain a reliable MIS designed to provide timely and forward-
looking information on the liquidity position of the bank and the group to the Board
and ALCO, both under normal and stress situations.
enriched_requirements.object: designed to provide timely and forward- looking information on the liquidity position of the bank and the group to the Board a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The MIS shall cover liquidity
positions in all currencies in which a bank conducts its business - both at the
bank level and on an aggregate Group basis.
enriched_requirements.object: all currencies in which a bank conducts its business - both at the bank level and on an aggregate Group basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
It shall capture all sources of
liquidity risk, including contingent risks and those arising from new activities, and
have the ability to furnish more granular and time sensitive information during
stress events.
enriched_requirements.object: liquidity risk, including contingent risks and those arising from new activities, and have the ability to furnish more granular

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Liquidity risk reports shall provide sufficient detail to enable management to
assess the sensitivity of a bank to changes in market conditions, its own financial
performance, and other important risk factors.
enriched_requirements.object: enable management to assess the sensitivity of a bank to changes in market conditions, its own financial performance, and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The reports shall inter alia include:
(1) cash flow projections and cash flow gaps;
enriched_requirements.object: cash flow projections and cash flow gaps

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall maintain appropriate internal controls, systems and procedures to
ensure adherence to liquidity risk management policies and procedure as also
adequacy of liquidity risk management functioning.
enriched_requirements.object: systems and procedures to ensure adherence to liquidity risk management policies and procedure as also adequac

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Top Management (direct reporting to the MD & CEO and / or the Board) shall
ensure that an independent party regularly reviews and evaluates the various
components of the bank's liquidity risk management process.

independent review process shall report key issues requiring immediate
attention, including instances of non-compliance to various guidance / limits for

concentration risk on the liability side of its balance sheet.

(i) IBL of a bank shall not exceed 200 per cent of its Net Worth as on
March 31 of the previous year.
enriched_requirements.object: of its Net Worth as on March 31 of the previous year

Board, fix a lower limit for its IBL, keeping in view its business
requirements.

(iii) The limit prescribed above shall include only fund-based IBL within

borrowing limit shall operate as a sub-limit within the above IBL limit.

A bank having high concentration of bulk deposits or deposits in excess of

volatile liabilities and contain the liquidity risk arising out of excessive

A bank shall estimate and monitor the cash flows arising from OBS exposures
(contingent liabilities) such as those related to special purpose vehicles (SPV),

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Securities lent in securities financing transactions shall be included in the
bank's assets if a bank retains beneficial ownership;
enriched_requirements.object: bank's assets if a bank retains beneficial ownership

conditions: if those securities do not appear on its balance sheets
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Securities received through collateral swaps shall not be included in a
bank's assets if those securities do not appear on its balance sheets;
enriched_requirements.object: bank's assets if those securities do not appear on its balance sheets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
<image>
(4) Where a bank has encumbered securities in repos or other securities
financing transactions but has retained beneficial ownership and the
securities remain on its balance sheet, such securities shall be allocated to
the appropriate RSF category according to its characteristics such as HQLA
classification, maturity and issuer.
enriched_requirements.object: appropriate RSF category according to its characteristics such as HQLA classification, maturity and issuer

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The treatment for the amount receivable by a bank under reverse repo
transaction shall be same as applicable to any other loan.
enriched_requirements.object: to any other loan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The applicable RSF
factor shall depend on the counterparty and term of the operation, subject to
following exceptions:
(1) Reverse repos with financial institutions that have residual maturity of less
than six months and secured by level 1 assets shall receive a 10 per cent
RSF factor as per paragraph 225.
enriched_requirements.object: and term of the operation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Reverse repos with financial institutions that have residual maturity of less
than six months and secured by other assets shall receive a 15 per cent
RSF factor as per paragraph 226.
enriched_requirements.object: cent RSF factor as per paragraph 226

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Amounts receivables and payable under securities financing transactions shall
be reported on a gross basis with the gross amount of such receivables and
payables reported on the RSF side and ASF side, respectively.
enriched_requirements.object: gross basis with the gross amount of such receivables and payables reported on the RSF side and ASF side, respectively

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The only
exception is securities financing transactions with a single counterparty which
may be reported on a net basis for NSFR calculation, provided the netting
conditions set out in Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, are met.
enriched_requirements.object: net basis for NSFR calculation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For loans which are only partially secured and are therefore separated into
secured and unsecured portions with different risk weights under Basel
Framework, the specific characteristics of these portions of loans shall be taken
into account for the calculation of the NSFR.
enriched_requirements.object: for the calculation of the NSFR

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The secured and unsecured
portions of a loan shall be treated according to its characteristics and assigned
the corresponding RSF factor.
enriched_requirements.object: its characteristics and assigned the corresponding RSF factor

conditions:
If it is not possible to draw the distinction between
the secured and unsecured part of the loan
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If it is not possible to draw the distinction between
the secured and unsecured part of the loan, the higher RSF factor shall apply to
the whole loan.
enriched_requirements.object: loan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where an
eligible bilateral netting contract is in place that meets the conditions as specified
in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Prudential Norms on
Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, the net replacement cost may be used for
the set of derivative exposures covered by the contract.
enriched_requirements.object: set of derivative exposures covered by the contract

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In calculating NSFR derivative assets (i.e., derivative assets - cash collateral
received as variation margin on derivative assets), collateral received in
connection with derivative contracts may not offset the positive replacement cost
amount, regardless of whether or not netting is permitted under the bank's
operative accounting or risk-based framework, unless it is received in the form of
cash variation margin and meets the conditions as specified in Reserve Bank of
India (Small Finance Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions,
enriched_requirements.object: cost amount, regardless of whether or not netting is permitted under the bank's operative accounting or risk-based framework

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any remaining balance sheet liability associated with (a) variation margin
received that does not meet the criteria above, or (b) initial margin received may
not offset derivative assets and shall be assigned a zero per cent ASF factor.
enriched_requirements.object: per cent ASF factor

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If an on-balance sheet asset is associated with collateral posted as initial margin
and is reflected as such to the extent under the bank's accounting framework,
the asset shall not be counted as an encumbered asset in the calculation of a
bank's RSF under NSFR to avoid double-counting.
enriched_requirements.object: encumbered asset in the calculation of a bank's RSF under NSFR to avoid double-counting

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For NSFR disclosure template and related instructions a bank shall refer to
relevant instructions in Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Financial
Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: in Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall carry out both the
analyses.
enriched_requirements.object: analyses

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The framework, both DGA and TGA, shall be applied to the bank-level position
of assets, liabilities and OBS items of a bank which are rate sensitive.
enriched_requirements.object: bank-level position of assets, liabilities and OBS items of a bank which are rate sensitive

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank
shall compute its IRR position in each currency applying the DGA and TGA to
the Rate Sensitive Assets (RSA) / Rate Sensitive Liabilities (RSL) items, where
either the assets, or liabilities in a currency constitute 5 per cent or more of bank's
global assets or global liabilities.
enriched_requirements.object: in each currency applying the DGA and TGA to the Rate Sensitive Assets (RSA) / Rate Sensitive Liabilities (RSL) items

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The IRR position in all other residual currencies
shall be computed separately on an aggregate basis.
enriched_requirements.object: an aggregate basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The methodologies specified in these Directions shall serve as a benchmark.

A
bank which has more sophisticated systems may continue its existing systems
but shall also adopt the DGA and TGA as supervisory reporting / disclosure
frameworks.
enriched_requirements.object: and TGA as supervisory reporting / disclosure frameworks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Gap Report shall be generated by grouping rate sensitive liabilities, assets
and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets according to residual maturity
or next repricing period, whichever is earlier.
enriched_requirements.object: rate sensitive liabilities, assets and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets according to residual maturity or next r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall use the traditional Gap analysis to measure the level of a bank's
exposure to IRR in terms of sensitivity of its NII to interest rate movements over
the horizon of analysis, which is usually one year.
enriched_requirements.object: analysis to measure the level of a bank's exposure to IRR in terms of sensitivity of its NII to interest rate movements over t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall bucket all RSA,
RSL, and OBS items as per residual maturity / re-pricing date in various time
buckets as per guidance provided in Annex-VII and report Interest Rate
Sensitivity statement provided in Annex-III.
enriched_requirements.object: and OBS items as per residual maturity / re-pricing date in various time buckets as per guidance provided in Annex-VII and report

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compute Earnings at
Risk (EaR) representing the loss of income under different interest rate scenarios
over a time horizon of one year.
enriched_requirements.object: (EaR) representing the loss of income under different interest rate scenarios over a time horizon of one year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For calculation of changes in MVE due to changes in the interest rates, market
values of RSA and RSL shall be used.

However, for simplicity, the book values
of the RSA and RSL (both inclusive of notional value of rate sensitive OBS items)
may be used as an approximation.
enriched_requirements.object: approximation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall bucket all RSA and RSL, including the rate sensitive OBS asset and
liabilities, as per residual maturity / re-pricing dates in various time buckets as
per the guidance provided in Annex-VII, and compute the Modified Duration Gap
(MDG).
enriched_requirements.object: RSL, including the rate sensitive OBS asset and liabilities, as per residual maturity / re-pricing dates in various time buckets as

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The MDG shall be used to evaluate the impact on the MVE of the bank
under different interest rate scenarios.
enriched_requirements.object: the impact on the MVE of the bank under different interest rate scenarios

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Under MDG framework a bank shall compute Modified Duration of RSA (MDA)
and Modified Duration of RSL (MDL).
enriched_requirements.object: RSA (MDA) and Modified Duration of RSL (MDL)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The MDG, reflecting the degree of duration mismatch in the RSA and RSL in a
bank's balance sheet, shall be calculated with the help of the following formula:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The estimated drop in MVE resulting from the prescribed interest rate shock shall
indicate the economic impact on a bank's equity, assuming the shock scenario
materialises.
enriched_requirements.object: on a bank's equity, assuming the shock scenario materialises

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This decline shall not be construed as an accounting loss as
banking book is not marked to market.
enriched_requirements.object: accounting loss as banking book is not marked to market

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, a bank that
has the capability to use market value of assets and liabilities of banking book
shall do so.

The market values of assets and liabilities shall be determined by
discounted cash flow method.
enriched_requirements.object: cash flow method

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
absolute notional amount of rate sensitive off-balance sheet items in each
time bucket shall be included as RSA if positive and as RSL if negative.
enriched_requirements.object: if positive and as RSL if negative

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The mid-point of each time bucket shall be taken as a proxy for the maturity
of assets and liabilities in that time bucket, except for those for which the
bank is able to compute modified duration on individual basis.
enriched_requirements.object: proxy for the maturity of assets and liabilities in that time bucket

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The requirement to determine the impact of IRR on the Market Value Equity
(MVE) shall be applicable across the bank's entire balance sheet, i.e., both the
<image>
banking and trading books.
enriched_requirements.object: bank's entire balance sheet, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This requirement shall be is in addition to the existing
guidelines for assessing capital adequacy requirement for interest rate sensitive
positions in the trading book and banking book (under Pillar II) separately.
enriched_requirements.object: to the existing guidelines for assessing capital adequacy requirement for interest rate sensitive positions in the trading book and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall be considered an outlier from a supervisory perspective (under Pillar
II) if the economic value of its banking book declines by more than 20 per cent of
its MVE due to a standardised interest rate shock of 200 basis points.
enriched_requirements.object: from a supervisory perspective (under Pillar II) if the economic value of its banking book declines by more than 20 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However,
for decline in the MVE due to standardised interest rate shock of 200 basis points
on the entire balance sheet, a bank shall not be subjected to outlier criteria.
enriched_requirements.object: criteria

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The 200 basis point shock shall be applied to the entire balance
sheet including the trading book.
enriched_requirements.object: entire balance sheet including the trading book

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall monitor its IRR positions and take
appropriate corrective action with reference to the stipulations in paragraph 274
of the direction for internal limits on volatility of MVE, i.e., percentage variation in
the MVE, and limits on individual gaps.
enriched_requirements.object: and take appropriate corrective action with reference to the stipulations in paragraph 274 of the direction for internal lim

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall prepare the Interest Rate Sensitivity (IRS) Statement, under TGA
and DGA, as per reporting format provided in Annex-III, bucketing all RSA and
RSL in following time buckets as per table below:
Sr.
enriched_requirements.object: Sensitivity (IRS) Statement, under TGA and DGA, as per reporting format provided in Annex-III, bucketing all RSA and RSL in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The RSA and RSL with fixed maturities shall be classified in the relevant time
buckets based on residual maturity / re-pricing dates.
enriched_requirements.object: relevant time buckets based on residual maturity / re-pricing dates

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For RSA and RSL items without fixed maturity or with embedded optionality (i.e.,
savings bank deposits, current account deposits and mortgage loans), a bank
shall undertake behavioural studies to assess the interest rate sensitivity
realistically.
enriched_requirements.object: assess the interest rate sensitivity realistically

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall have an appropriate process and a detailed
framework for conducting such studies and review their output at least annually.
enriched_requirements.object: and a detailed framework for conducting such studies and review their output at least annually

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall apply the results of the behavioural studies on a consistent basis
and the results shall be reviewed / revised once a year in the first quarter of the
financial year, if necessary.
enriched_requirements.object: the behavioural studies on a consistent basis and the results shall be reviewed / revised once a year in the first quarter of the financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The behavioural studies shall be based on at least
three years data.
enriched_requirements.object: least three years data

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall evolve a suitable mechanism, supported by
empirical studies and behavioural analysis to estimate the future behaviour of
assets and liabilities and OBS items with respect to changes in market variables.
enriched_requirements.object: supported by empirical studies and behavioural analysis to estimate the future behaviour of assets and liabilities and OB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, a bank having the
capability to compute the MD of each item of RSA and RSL shall do so.

However, a bank which is not equipped to compute the MD of each individual
rate sensitive asset, liability and OBS position shall:
(1) group RSA and RSL under the broad categories indicated in Annex-VII
under various time buckets;
enriched_requirements.object: and RSL under the broad categories indicated in Annex-VII under various time buckets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The MDG computed from this approach shall be simpler, providing a cost
effective alternative despite approximations in the calculation of MD.
enriched_requirements.object: cost effective alternative despite approximations in the calculation of MD

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compile the ALM statements and compute the MDG for the entire
balance sheet, encompassing both the banking and trading books.
enriched_requirements.object: and compute the MDG for the entire balance sheet, encompassing both the banking and trading books

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
While reporting the currency-wise IRR position, as advised in paragraph 252
above, in Interest Rate Sensitivity statement provided in Annex-III, a bank shall
convert the foreign currencies into ₹ using the relevant spot closing rates as
published by FEDAI.
enriched_requirements.object: into ₹ using the relevant spot closing rates as published by FEDAI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compute the MD of each item or group of items
of rate sensitive assets, liabilities and OBS items using the appropriate coupon
and relevant foreign currency yield curve.
enriched_requirements.object: each item or group of items of rate sensitive assets, liabilities and OBS items using the appropriate coupon and relevant foreign cu

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For residual currencies, a bank may
use the appropriate coupon and yield curve of the largest among the residual
currencies.
enriched_requirements.object: and yield curve of the largest among the residual currencies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In deciding the appropriate coupon and yield curves, a bank shall
follow the principles outlined in Annex-VII.
enriched_requirements.object: in Annex-VII

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall, with the approval of its Board / RMC, set appropriate internal limits
on Earnings at Risk (EaR) and volatility in the MVE, based on its risk bearing and
risk management capacity.
enriched_requirements.object: of its Board / RMC, set appropriate internal limits on Earnings at Risk (EaR) and volatility in the MVE, based on its risk bearing

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These limits shall be linked to MVE for DGA and the
NII for TGA.
enriched_requirements.object: for DGA and the NII for TGA

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board / ALCO shall periodically review the above limits after
assessing various scenarios of interest rates and the resultant volatility of
earnings in terms of NII and volatility in Net Worth.
enriched_requirements.object: limits after assessing various scenarios of interest rates and the resultant volatility of earnings in terms of NII and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO prior approval shall be obtained for deciding upon yields, assumptions
used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies, etc., for DGA.
enriched_requirements.object: upon yields, assumptions used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank
shall ensure that such decisions are compliant with extant regulatory guidelines.
enriched_requirements.object: are compliant with extant regulatory guidelines

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall update material assumptions on a regular basis to reflect the current
market and operating environment.
enriched_requirements.object: a regular basis to reflect the current market and operating environment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The process for developing such material
assumptions shall be formalized and reviewed at least annually.
enriched_requirements.object: at least annually

conditions:
if an interest rate shock of 200
basis points results in a drop in the value of equity of more than 20 per cent of
MVE
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In accordance with Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, if an interest rate shock of 200
basis points results in a drop in the value of equity of more than 20 per cent of
MVE, the level of IRR shall be treated as excessive.

In such cases, the RBI may
require the bank to take corrective action as specified in the above Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: take corrective action as specified in the above Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall measure its vulnerability to losses in stressed market conditions,
including the breakdown of key assumptions, and consider these results when
establishing and reviewing its limits and policies in respect of interest rate risk, in
<image>
accordance with the instructions provided in Reserve Bank of India (Small
Finance Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: losses in stressed market conditions, including the breakdown of key assumptions, and consider these results when establi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall adopt the practice of periodic model validation.
enriched_requirements.object: periodic model validation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where
internal models / software packages are used, the bank shall ensure the integrity
and validation of data / assumptions being used to generate the results,
validation and functioning of the entire system of IRR management by an
independent audit either by an experienced internal auditor or external auditor
who is conversant with risk management processes.
enriched_requirements.object: validation of data / assumptions being used to generate the results, validation and functioning of the entire system of IRR m

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Audit Committee of the
Board (ACB) shall ensure the suitability of auditors through a due diligence
process.
enriched_requirements.object: auditors through a due diligence process

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure documentation in respect of discount rates, coupons,
assumptions used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies, and
validation process.
enriched_requirements.object: of discount rates, coupons, assumptions used / proposed to be used, bucketing, behavioural studies, and validation pro

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All material assumptions, regardless of the source, shall be
supported with analysis and documentation.
enriched_requirements.object: and documentation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall ensure that sufficient
documentation is made available at the time of validation, internal audit, statutory
audit, and RBI inspection.
enriched_requirements.object: is made available at the time of validation, internal audit, statutory audit, and RBI inspection

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall prepare domestic structural liquidity statement (SLS)- ₹ on a daily
basis.
enriched_requirements.object: statement (SLS)- ₹ on a daily basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall report the liquidity return comprising the SLS in four parts, viz.,
(1) Domestic currency for Indian operations
(2) Foreign currency for Indian operations
(3) Combined Indian operations
(4) Consolidated bank operations
The formats of the returns are provided in Annex-I.
enriched_requirements.object: comprising the SLS in four parts, viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall adhere to the periodicity of submission in respect of each part of the
Liquidity Return is specified in the table below.
enriched_requirements.object: of submission in respect of each part of the Liquidity Return is specified in the table below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, bank shall refer Reserve
Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024 dated February
27, 2024 for submission timelines for all regulatory returns.
enriched_requirements.object: India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024 dated February 27, 2024 for submission timelines for all regulatory retur

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit the Basel III Liquidity Returns (BLR) at the frequencies
specified in the table below.
enriched_requirements.object: Liquidity Returns (BLR) at the frequencies specified in the table below

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall report LCR and NSFR positions as on
date of reporting in monthly BLR-1 and quarterly BLR-6, respectively.
enriched_requirements.object: positions as on date of reporting in monthly BLR-1 and quarterly BLR-6, respectively

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the stipulated periodicity of submission of statement on LCR to
Reserve Bank, a bank shall develop operational capacity to increase the
periodicity to weekly or even daily in stressed situations if so directed by RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: increase the periodicity to weekly or even daily in stressed situations if so directed by RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
The time lag in reporting shall be as short as feasible, notwithstanding the outer
limits prescribed.
enriched_requirements.object: feasible, notwithstanding the outer limits prescribed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, bank shall refer Reserve Bank of India (Filing of
Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory
returns
Sl.
enriched_requirements.object: India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024, for submission timelines for all regulatory returns Sl

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall prepare the IRS Statement, under TGA and DGA, as per reporting
format provided in Annex-III.
enriched_requirements.object: under TGA and DGA, as per reporting format provided in Annex-III

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit IRS Statement under TGA and DGA approaches on a
monthly basis.
enriched_requirements.object: TGA and DGA approaches on a monthly basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, bank shall refer Reserve Bank of India (Filing of
Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024 for submission timelines for all regulatory
returns.
enriched_requirements.object: India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions, 2024 for submission timelines for all regulatory returns

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and
guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions

continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.

The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
